statement.focus_question: In other words, given a permutation $x$ of $\mathbb{N}$ must there be indices with either $i<j<k<l$ or $i>j>k>l$ such that $x_i,x_j,x_k,x_l$ are an arithmetic progression?
statement.normalized: Must every permutation of $\mathbb{N}$ contain a monotone 4-term arithmetic progression? In other words, given a permutation $x$ of $\mathbb{N}$ must there be indices with either $i<j<k<l$ or $i>j>k>l$ such that $x_i,x_j,x_k,x_l$ are an arithmetic progression?
statement.original: Must every permutation of $\mathbb{N}$ contain a monotone 4-term arithmetic progression? In other words, given a permutation $x$ of $\mathbb{N}$ must there be indices with either $i<j<k<l$ or $i>j>k>l$ such that $x_i,x_j,x_k,x_l$ are an arithmetic progression?
